fund.name: ICICI Prudential Nifty 50 Index Fund - Direct Growth
fund.display_name: ICICI Prudential Nifty 50 Index Fund - Direct Growth
fund.fund_house: ICICI Prudential Asset Management Company Limited

allocations.rationale: The Nifty 50 index captures India's largest, most liquid, and most growth-oriented companies. As a high-risk-tolerant investor aiming for 15Cr in 25 years, this fund aligns with your desire to ride India's large-cap growth story while keeping costs low through a direct plan.

fund.name: Motilal Oswal Nifty Midcap 150 Index Fund - Direct Growth
fund.display_name: Motilal Oswal Nifty Midcap 150 Index Fund - Direct Growth
fund.fund_house: Motilal Oswal Asset Management Company Limited

allocations.rationale: Mid-cap stocks are historically more volatile but offer higher growth potential — something you're willing to chase for FIRE by 45. This fund gives you pure exposure to mid-cap India's most promising growth stories.

fund.name: Parag Parikh Flexi Cap Fund - Direct Growth
fund.display_name: Parag Parikh Flexi Cap Fund - Direct Growth

allocations.rationale: Parag Parikh is a genius in small-cap and mid-cap alpha capture. His fund is one of the few flexi-cap funds that boldly invests in unlisted private equity and small-cap stocks — perfect for your desire to build alpha from India's best entrepreneurial ideas.

fund.name: Motilal Oswal India Equity Opportunities Fund - Direct Growth
fund.display_name: Motilal Oswal India Equity Opportunities Fund - Direct Growth
fund.fund_house: Motilal Oswal Asset Management Company Limited

allocations.rationale: This is the "best of both worlds" fund — it invests in large-cap stalwarts to ride macro growth and medium-cap ideas to capture alpha. You're an aggressive investor, and this fund is built for people who want to be fully exposed to India's growth story.

fund.name: Franklin Templeton India Equity Fund - Direct Growth
fund.display_name: Franklin Templeton India Equity Fund - Direct Growth

allocations.rationale: Franklin Templeton is a global powerhouse with decades of India investing experience. This fund is a "buy-and-hold" large-cap India fund — essential as a stable floor under your aggressive portfolio.

fund.name: Franklin India Bluechip Fund - Direct Growth
fund.display_name: Franklin India Bluechip Fund - Direct Growth

allocations.rationale: This is one of India's oldest large-cap funds — it's a conservative foundation for your aggressive portfolio. You need this as a "floor" to reduce stress during market corrections and ensure you don't panic-sell.

fund.name: Motilal Oswal Global Equity Fund - Direct Growth
fund.display_name: Motilal Oswal Global Equity Fund - Direct Growth
fund.fund_house: Motilal Oswal Asset Management Company Limited

allocations.rationale: You're interested in international diversification — this fund gives you pure exposure to global growth stories (S&P 500, Nasdaq, UK, EU, etc.) — a critical hedge against India-specific volatility and a way to ride global tech and AI booms.

plan.setup_phase: Your initial corpus of ₹5,000,000 will be deployed in lumpsum across all 7 funds in the first month. You're investing ₹200,000 per month — ₹70,000 into the Nifty 50 index fund, ₹40,000 each into the mid-cap and flexi-cap funds, ₹15,000 into the India Equity Opportunities fund, and ₹7,500 each into Franklin India Bluechip and Motilal Oswal Global Equity. All funds are direct plans to avoid wealth tax on fund managers.
review_checkpoints: 6 months — see how the SIPs are tracking and ensure you're not missing any installments
1 year — check the fund performance and verify if the actual returns align with your expectations
3 years — re-evaluate the fund portfolio and check if you want to shift the asset allocation slightly
5 years — re-evaluate the fund portfolio and consider shifting a small portion into debt for emergency funds if needed
10 years — ensure you're on track for your startup funding goal (you can still shift some funds lock into a debt fund for this purpose)
plan.rebalancing_guidelines: Do not rebalance for the first 10 years — let the aggressive growth ride. After 10 years, consider shifting 10-15% of your portfolio into debt if you're feeling the need for emergency funds or you want to lock a portion for your startup. At 15 years, re-evaluate the allocation to ensure it's still aggressive enough for your FIRE goal. At 20 years, review the fund house performance and consider rotating into index funds (if you're not already there) to keep costs low. At 25 years, your portfolio should be 90% equity-index funds + 10% global equity.
plan.rationale: You're a 30-year-old tech lead with a high-risk appetite, aiming to retire by 45 and build a 15Cr portfolio. You're not scared of volatility — in fact, you're leveraging it. Your plan is to ride India's growth story through large-cap index funds, mid-cap alpha, and flexi-cap small-cap investing while hedging with global equity. The ₹5,000,000 corpus is deployed lumpsum to catch the first big growth wave, while the monthly SIP of ₹200,000 compounds aggressively over 25 years. You're also saving for your startup — ideally, after 10 years, lock 10-15% of your portfolio into a debt fund or a separate account to fund it without touching the core FIRE corpus. This plan is designed to be aggressive, cost-efficient, and diversified across India's growth drivers and global opportunities.
risks: Market volatility can cause short-term pain — you must be mentally prepared to ride through corrections
plan.disclaimer: For research/educational purposes only. Not certified financial advice.

sip_step_up.description: Increase SIP by 10% every year to match salary growth

allocation_schedule.trigger: First major market correction and capital preservation need
Second major market correction and capital preservation need
Final FIRE phase — lock down capital and avoid panic selling

plan.perspective: Greed is good — if you're not scared of volatility, your goal is to ride the biggest growth waves in India and the world. This plan is built for someone who wants to be fully exposed to India's growth story through large-cap index funds and aggressive mid-cap/small-cap alpha, while diversifying into international opportunities for global tech and AI booms. It's a FIRE plan, but also a personal asset-building plan — your goal is not just to retire early, but to build a legacy of wealth that's large enough to fund your life and your startup. You're not looking to be safe — you're looking to be bold.

aps.reasoning: EQUITY PASSIVE FRACTION: 60% passive (40% Nifty 50 + 20% Midcap 150 Index) out of 95% equity allocation. TURNOVER: Very passive - no rebalancing for first 10 years, only strategic shifts every 5-10 years. COST EMPHASIS: Moderate-high - explicit mention of direct plans to avoid wealth tax and cost efficiency focus. RESEARCH VS COST: Mixed - some emphasis on fund-specific research (Parag Parikh alpha capture) but also broad market exposure. TIME HORIZON: Very passive - 25-year horizon with infrequent intervention. PORTFOLIO ACTIVENESS: Moderately active - while 60% is passive index funds, the remaining 35% equity is in actively managed funds with positive alpha and tracking error >1%, plus global equity exposure.

pqs.reasoning: Goal Alignment (0.9): Excellent alignment with investor's aggressive FIRE goals, high equity exposure preference, and international diversification interest. The 25-year horizon supports the aggressive equity-heavy allocation. Diversification (0.7): Good domestic diversification across large/mid/small caps but limited international exposure (only 5%). Heavy concentration in Indian equities (95%) despite global fund availability. Risk-Return Appropriateness (0.8): Appropriate aggressive stance for investor profile, but projected returns (21-23% for equity funds) may be optimistic based on historical market performance. Internal Consistency (0.6): Contradictions exist - rationale mentions 'no debt' but allocation schedule shows debt allocation starting at year 10; rebalancing guidelines conflict with allocation schedule; multiple overlapping funds (3 large-cap focused funds). Tax Efficiency (0.9): Excellent for new_regime investor - all funds are equity-oriented (≥65% equity) benefiting from favorable equity taxation, no ELSS recommendation (appropriate for new regime), and direct plans minimize costs.
